assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md contains '## Classification: CODE CHANGE REQUEST' and 'This requires a code change and a sub-task is created.' The report table shows '30001 | CODE CHANGE REQUEST | Sub-task created'. subtask-30001.md exists with the transaction wrapping sub-task.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains '## Classification: SUGGESTION' and the Reasoning section states: 'The reviewer uses suggestive language: "should also add" and "would help." This proposes an improvement (adding a database index) that is not required for functional correctness of the feature.'

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md contains '## Classification: NIT' and 'No sub-task is created for nits.' No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md contains '## Classification: QUESTION' and 'No sub-task is created for questions.' No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1), '## Description' (line 10), '## Files to Modify' (line 13), '## Implementation Notes' (line 16), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 23). This is the only sub-task created.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' (line 7) followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 8).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' (line 29) with 'Comment ID: 30001', 'Author: reviewer-a', 'File: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (line 60)', and the full original comment text quoted verbatim.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md), one sub-task description (subtask-30001.md), and a report (report.md). No code files (.rs, .toml, .sql, etc.) appear in the outputs. The report itself documents only verification findings without code modifications.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have individual classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report's Review Comment Classifications table lists all four comments with their classifications. The report references findings from all four comments in its narrative sections.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report's overall result is 'FAIL' with issues requiring attention. There is no mention of merging, auto-merging, or any merge action in the report. The report concludes with findings summaries and sub-task creation, not merge actions.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: The report table contains '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file adding 5 integration tests |'. The Style/Conventions section also states 'Test Change Classification: ADDITIVE -- tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file'.

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a detailed '### Convention Upgrade Eligibility' section evaluating four criteria: (1) CONVENTIONS.md check finding no index creation conventions, (2) Codebase pattern check finding zero occurrences of index creation patterns, (3) Performance-related scrutiny finding no performance conventions, (4) General best practices being insufficient. The report's Style/Conventions section also states 'Convention Upgrade: PASS -- comment 30002 (index suggestion) not upgraded; no CONVENTIONS.md backing or codebase pattern'.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory. review-30002.md concludes: 'Since no documented or demonstrated project convention supports this suggestion, it remains classified as SUGGESTION. No sub-task is created.' The report table shows '30002 | SUGGESTION | No sub-task (no convention backing)'.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: The report's Test Quality row states 'Eval Quality: N/A'. The Style/Conventions section states 'Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews found'. The Test Quality check is 'PASS' indicating Eval Quality (N/A) did not negatively affect it.
